entries.title: Quadratic Formula
entries.content: Solves any quadratic equation ax² + bx + c = 0 where a ≠ 0. The discriminant b² - 4ac determines the nature of roots: positive gives two real roots, zero gives one repeated root, negative gives complex roots.

entries.title: Difference of Squares
entries.content: A factoring pattern for binomials that are the difference of two perfect squares. Useful for simplifying expressions and solving equations quickly.

entries.title: Exponent Rules
entries.content: Rules for simplifying expressions with powers. When multiplying same base, add exponents; when dividing, subtract; power of a power multiplies exponents; negative exponent means reciprocal.

entries.title: Logarithm Definition
entries.content: A logarithm is the inverse operation of exponentiation. log_b(x) = y means b^y = x. Natural log uses base e; common log uses base 10.
entries.formula: log_b(x) = y ⟺ b^y = x; ln(e) = 1; log(10) = 1

entries.title: Systems of Linear Equations
entries.content: Two or more linear equations solved together. Methods: substitution, elimination, or matrix methods. A unique solution exists when lines intersect at one point; no solution if parallel; infinitely many if coincident.
entries.example: 2x + y = 7 and x - y = 2 → x = 3, y = 1
